title: World's largest scorpion had 6-inch pincers, and prowled UK land and waters 415 million years ago
summary: Enigmatic 415 million-year-old fossils belong to a giant scorpion that may have reached lengths of around 3 feet (1 meter), a remarkable body size because most life on land at that time was small.

title: The US Has a Plan to Combat Screwworm. It Involves a Lot More Flies
summary: Releasing sterilized flies can crash a local population of flesh-eating screwworms. But the US currently has limited capacity to produce them.

title: 13 Environmentally Conscious Packing Tips for Your Next Vacation
summary: Your trip starts impacting the planet before you even leave home. Here are a few pointers for keeping your footprint small.

title: Venus and Jupiter are about to almost touch in the sky in one of 2026's best skywatching events
summary: A close conjunction of the two brightest planets in the night sky will take place over several evenings, with the best time to look being June 9-11.

title: Some 'extinct' volcanoes may just be going through a growth spurt, before they 'wake up in this catastrophic stage,' emerging research suggests
summary: A volcano that erupted after being asleep for more than 100,000 years is leading more volcanologists to say we must redefine volcano activity to ensure eruptions don't surprise us.

title: Scientists race to collect the last seeds from a critically endangered tree before it goes extinct
summary: Seeds from the last surviving wild Dendroseris neriifolia tree are now stored in Kew Gardens' Millennium Seed Bank as researchers work to find ways to reintroduce the species into the wild.

title: Therabody Promo Codes: 15% Off June 2026
summary: Save on the science-backed devices you’ve been eyeing with 15% off Theragun discount code and 30% off other great deals.

title: New Velociraptor cousin was a '4-winged' dragon that hunted prey from the trees of ancient China, fossil find hints
summary: A new microraptor from Cretaceous China likely preyed on ancient birds.

title: Italian teenagers discover 1,800-year-old Roman luxury house underneath their high school gym
summary: After being notified by mischievous high school students, archaeologists uncovered a large and luxurious second-century Roman house near the Colosseum.

title: James Webb telescope detects most distant dormant black hole, invisible in all wavelengths and weighing as much as 6 billion suns
summary: JWST found a black hole hiding in a galaxy more than 10 billion light-years away from Earth, and used a cosmic magnifying glass to determine its mass.

title: Satellite images reveals mangroves rebounding worldwide — but here's why they could still 'drown'
summary: A new study finds mangrove forests are no longer shrinking worldwide, offering hope for coastal protection and climate resilience. But other research warns sea level rise could reduce their ability to store carbon.

title: Mysterious repeating radio signal traced to 'vampire' star that's slowly eating its companion
summary: Radio astronomers have decoded a peculiar repeating radio signal from deep space, tracing it to a vampiric binary star system.

title: Archaeologists study the International Space Station and Everest to figure out 'how humans adapt in this impossible place where we have no business going'
summary: Archaeologists are turning their attention and research skills to far-flung places on the Earth and beyond, discovering new information about how humans survive in extreme environments.

title: 'Cannibal' CME from rare 'anti-Hale' sunspot will slam into Earth today, bringing auroras to 23 US states
summary: Northern lights are projected across the Northern U.S. and Europe tonight as Earth gets hit by a strong solar eruption.

title: Daddy longlegs may be capturing and devouring frogs in the tropical forests of South America
summary: Daddy longlegs have been observed eating frogs in South America, suggesting that these arachnids may be predators of vertebrates.

title: Get the legendary Celestron NexStar 8SE telescope at its lowest price of the year in this early Amazon Prime Day deal
summary: Our telescope expert chose the Celestron NexStar 8SE as our best overall telescope, and now, with $200 off, it makes this versatile telescope excellent value for money ahead of Prime Day.

title: 'Totally counterintuitive': Scientists accidentally discover magnetic fields around 7 distant planets, opening new window in the search for life
summary: Scientists accidentally discover magnetic fields around 7 distant planets

title: Physicists achieve 'perfect randomness' for the first time ever
summary: Physicists used quantum bits to achieve "perfect randomness" in a world-first experiment. The results of their research could strengthen cryptography and other security systems.

title: 17,000-year-old stripes of red in a Welsh cave are the oldest rock art in the UK, study finds
summary: Over a century after a red-lined cave wall was discovered, scientists have determined that it represents the U.K.'s oldest rock art.

title: Rusty, orange water 'bleeds' across brilliant Bolivian lagoon — Earth from space
summary: A 2015 astronaut photo shows dark-orange water that appears to bleed across the bright-white floor of a high-altitude salt lake in the Bolivian Andes.

title: NASA confirms meteor exploded over northeastern US with force of 230 tons of TNT
summary: NASA shared an initial analysis of a 5-foot-wide fireball meteor that exploded in the sky over the northeastern U.S. on Saturday, May 30.

title: Astronauts could use lightning-like plasma jets to kill germs on the moon and Mars, demo hints
summary: A new lab experiment is testing plasma jets as a water-free solution for "space laundry" on future missions to the moon and Mars.

title: First whole-genome sequence of a Greenland shark holds clues to their extreme longevity
summary: A genomic study of Greenland sharks, thought to be the longest-lived vertebrates on the planet, is hinting at the secrets to their epic lifespan and cancer resistance.

title: Scientists reveal the origin of the Euphrates — a river that fed the 'cradle of civilization'
summary: The Euphrates River fueled the "cradle of civilization," and a new study reveals the waterway was born of two other ancient rivers around 3.6 million years ago.

title: 'We were being bullied in our own home': How 'authoritarian' HOAs are contributing to the insect apocalypse
summary: In the book "Bitter Honey," writer and researcher Jennie Durant explores how industrial agriculture is destroying bees — and what can be done to stop them.

title: Bronze Age 5-year-old's skull found in Uzbekistan is the oldest known evidence of surgery in Central Asia
summary: A child's 4,000-year-old skull found in Uzbekistan has signs of trepanation, making it the oldest evidence of surgery in Central Asia on record.

title: 'Astonishing': James Webb telescope spots the most chemically primitive galaxy in the ancient universe
summary: The James Webb telescope peered into an ancient spot of light, and found it to be the most metal-poor galaxy in the early universe.

title: Astronomers gaze into the 'Crystal Ball Nebula' and see a vision of our dying sun — Space photo of the week
summary: The Crystal Ball Nebula has captivated astronomers for more than 200 years, and it offers a bittersweet glimpse of a dying star system similar to our own.

title: How many generations of humans have there been?
summary: Modern humans have been around 300,000 years. How many generations is that?

title: من واشنطن إلى أوتاوا.. الأميركيون يبحثون عن جواز بديل
summary: رغم التوتر الذي شاب العلاقات ​الأميركية الكندية في عهد الرئيس الأميركي دونالد ترامب، تشير بيانات موافقات الحصول ​على الجنسية الكندية ضمن مجموعة ⁠واسعة من القواعد الحديثة إلى أن عددا متزايدا من الأميركيين يرحبون بفكرة الحصول على الجنسية الكندية.

title: بيان رسمي مصري بعد حادثة اصطياد نوع نادر من أسماك القرش
summary: أصدر المعهد القومي المصري لعلوم البحار والمصائد، السبت، بيانا بشأن حادثة اصطياد نوع نادر من أسماك القرش في منطقة القصير بالبحر الأحمر.

title: دراسة تكشف "سر مثلث برمودا" الذي حير العلماء
summary: كشف علماء من مؤسسة كارنيغي للعلوم وجامعة ييل، عن تفسير جديد للغز الجيولوجي المرتبط بجزر برمودا، بعد اكتشاف بنية صخرية ضخمة مخفية تحت قاع المحيط الأطلسي ساهمت في إبقاء الجزيرة مرتفعة فوق سطح البحر منذ ملايين السنين.
